RE:
Dear si,r:
Request for coDflict of Interest opinion
we hereby request, on behaff of Sacranento county
Supervisor sandra snoley, a written opinion advising
whether she is required to disqualify herself fron
participating i.n a matter to be heard by the Board of
Supervisors on August 19 , L992.
The confLict of interest issue arises from a recent
proposal by I'lercy HeaLthcare Sacramento, to establish a
trauma center at its Mercy-Ameri,can River Hospital . 
The
proposal of Mercy Healthcare Sacramento was made in
response to a Request For Proposals issued by the County
of Sacramento. Currently, the proposal, is under review
and evaluation by County staff. 
on August 19, L992, t}j.e
proposal wiLl- be presented to the Board of Supervisors of
the County for a deterrnination of whether or not the
county should enter into a contract, and provide funding,
for such trauma center services by Mercy Healthcare
Sacramento .
currently, the University of California at Davis is
the only provider of trauma center servj-ces in the county
of Sacramento. Said services are provided pursuant to a
contract with the county. Any contract with l"lercy
Healthcare sacranento for trauma center services would be
suppl-emental to contract services provided by the
University of California. 
The University currently
admits approximately 4,000 trauna patients per year. If
the County enters into the contract with Mercy
Healthcare, approximately 2,000 of those 4,000 annual
trauma patients would be diverted to l'lercy Healthcare.


Fair Political Practices
Commiss ion
June 26, L992
The University of California has taken a public position
supporting the development of a second trauma center at Mercy-
American River Hospital-. This office has been advised by the
staff at the County Health Department that certain consequences
result where the University is the sole provider of trauma center
servlces in the county. The Unj-versity has expressed concern
with overcrowding resulting from a percej-ved excess of trauma
patients at its hospital. 
Moreover, the extremely high vol-ume of
trauma patients in relation to patients undergoing elective
surgery has educational and financial repercussions. surgical
training at the University hospital involves an undue emphasis on
trauma injuries and a correspondingly insufficient opportunity
for other kinds of surgical training. 
Accordingly, the
University hospital is less satisfactory as a teaching facility.
In addition, there is historical evidence that, as opposed to
patients undergoing elective surgery, trauma patients are less
able to pay for their medical care.
Ms. Smoley has no financial interest in Mercy Healthcare
Sacramento. However, by virtue of her husband's interests in an
architectural firm, she does have an exi,sting f j,nancial interest
in the University of california. 
That is, within the past 12
months her husband, or his f irrn, has received pa)rment for
architectural services from the University in sufficient amounts
to constitute a financial interest within the meani-ng of the Act.
By your advice letter No. I-89-467, dated May 4, 1990, you
described the circumstances v/herein Ms. Smoley is prohibited from
participating in decisions which affect clients of her husband's
architectural f irrn. More specifically, that letter advised that
she may not participate in any decision she knows or has reason
to know wiLl have a reasonably foreseeable fj.nancial effect,
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on any
ctient of her husband's architectural f irrn who has provided
income to such firrn of $2,L74 or more in the 12 months prior to
the time r^rhen the decision is nade.
Based upon your letter, we have advised that until 12 months
foJ,Iowing the receipt by Ms. Smoley's husband, personally, of
$500 or by his architectural firrn of $2,174, for services to the
University of California, Ms. smoley nust disqualify herself from
any decision of the Board of Supervisors in which the University,
though not a subject of the proceeding, wiII incur an increase or
decrease in gross revenues, assets, or liabilities 
in the amount
of S1 million or more, or an increase or decrease in expenditures
of $25O,OOO or more. (2 CaI. Adnin. Code 18702.5 (a)).


Fair PoIitica1 Practices
Cornmiss ion
June 26, 1992
Based upon the above-described facts, we have concluded that
the Unj-versj.ty would not be the subject of the proceeding to
deterrnine whether a second trauma center should be designated at
Iilercy-American River Hospital. However, we have advised that it
is reasonably foreseeable that such a decision, made in the face
of express support by the University, could have a substantial
financial effect on the University.
We do not have specific facts which would indicate that the
expenditures will result in an increase or decrease of the
University's gross revenues, assets or liabilities 
in the amount
of $1 nillion or more, or will increase or decrease in the
University's expenditures by $250,o0o or more. We have, however,
advised Ms. Snol-ey that she should disqualify herself from
participating in any such proceedings regarding the designation
of the second trauma facilj.ty at Mercy-American River Hospital
unless she can obtain frorn the University of California written
verification that the designation of a second trauma center would
not affect the University's expenditures on revenues in amounts
in excess of $250,o00 or $1 rnillion respectively.
Ms. Srnoley desires a further opinion from the Fair Political
Practices Cornrnission and has a need for a response prior to the
August L9, 1992 hearing.
If I can be of further assistance, please contact ne at
(915) 440-5s58.
very truly yours,
cc: Sandra Smoley

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Sacramento County Supervisor Sandra Smoley must disqualify herself from participating in a Board of Supervisors decision regarding a proposed trauma center contract with Mercy Healthcare Sacramento, given her husband's financial interests in the University of California through his architectural firm.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from Sacramento County Counsel to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present in standard format. The document details a conflict of interest situation and references a prior FPPC advice letter (I-89-467) but does not contain FPPC's response to this request. Requestor indicates need for response prior to August 19, 1992 hearing.

embedding.summary: County Counsel requests an FPPC opinion on whether Supervisor Sandra Smoley should disqualify herself from a Board decision on a trauma center contract proposal, due to her husband's architectural firm having received payments from UC Davis, which has a financial interest in the outcome.
